prompts.question: Many students choose to study online because it gives them more flexibility.
prompts.targetPattern: It is increasingly common for + noun + to + verb
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 IELTS-style sentences using the target pattern about online learning.

prompts.question: University tuition has become very expensive in many countries.
prompts.targetPattern: One of the main reasons why + clause + is that + clause
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 academic sentences explaining a cause using the target pattern.

prompts.question: Smartphones have changed the way people communicate every day.
prompts.targetPattern: Thanks to + noun, + subject + can now + verb
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 IELTS-style sentences using the target pattern about technology in daily life.

prompts.question: Artificial intelligence is starting to replace some human jobs in modern industries.
prompts.targetPattern: While + clause, it is also true that + clause
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 balanced academic sentences using the target pattern about AI and jobs.

prompts.question: Air pollution in large cities affects people's health every year.
prompts.targetPattern: A growing number of + plural noun + are + verb-ing
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 IELTS-style sentences using the target pattern about pollution in cities.

prompts.question: Governments are encouraging citizens to use renewable energy at home.
prompts.targetPattern: In order to + verb, governments should + verb
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 IELTS-style sentences using the target pattern about renewable energy policy.

prompts.question: More employees are working from home since the pandemic began.
prompts.targetPattern: There has been a significant rise in + noun
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 IELTS-style sentences using the target pattern about remote work.

prompts.question: Long working hours can harm employees' mental and physical health.
prompts.targetPattern: Not only + auxiliary + subject + verb, but + subject + also + verb
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 academic sentences using the target pattern about working hours.

prompts.question: Regular exercise helps people stay healthy and avoid common illnesses.
prompts.targetPattern: By + verb-ing, people can + verb
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 IELTS-style sentences using the target pattern about healthy habits.

prompts.question: Many young people today eat too much fast food and not enough vegetables.
prompts.targetPattern: Despite + noun phrase, + clause
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 academic sentences using the target pattern about diet and lifestyle.

prompts.question: Local governments often invest in public parks and libraries.
prompts.targetPattern: It is the responsibility of + noun + to + verb
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 IELTS-style sentences using the target pattern about government duties.

prompts.question: Higher taxes on unhealthy food may reduce obesity rates.
prompts.targetPattern: If + subject + were to + verb, + subject + would + verb
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 academic sentences using the target pattern about taxation and health.

prompts.question: Young people today often live far from their parents because of work.
prompts.targetPattern: Compared with + noun, + clause
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 IELTS-style sentences using the target pattern about family life today.

prompts.question: Volunteering helps build stronger communities and personal skills.
prompts.targetPattern: Not only does + subject + verb, but it also + verb
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 academic sentences using the target pattern about volunteering.

prompts.question: CCTV cameras in public places may help reduce crime.
prompts.targetPattern: One possible solution to + noun + is to + verb
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 IELTS-style sentences using the target pattern about crime prevention.

prompts.question: Some people argue that prison is not the most effective way to reduce repeat offences.
prompts.targetPattern: Rather than + verb-ing, + subject + should + verb
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 academic sentences using the target pattern about punishment and rehabilitation.

prompts.question: Traffic jams in big cities waste a lot of people's time every day.
prompts.targetPattern: One of the biggest problems facing + noun + is + noun
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 IELTS-style sentences using the target pattern about urban transport.

prompts.question: Cycling to work is becoming more popular in many cities.
prompts.targetPattern: Encouraging people to + verb + could + verb
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 IELTS-style sentences using the target pattern about sustainable transport.

prompts.question: Many people now read the news on their phones instead of newspapers.
prompts.targetPattern: A large number of + plural noun + tend to + verb
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 IELTS-style sentences using the target pattern about how people consume news.

prompts.question: Social media influencers have a strong effect on young people's choices.
prompts.targetPattern: It can be argued that + clause
prompts.instruction: Write 2–3 academic sentences using the target pattern about influencers and society.

prompts.question: Some people believe that online learning is as effective as traditional classroom learning. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words. Include a topic sentence, explanation, example, and result.

prompts.question: Some governments believe that university education should be free for all citizens. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of this policy.
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words focusing on either an advantage or a disadvantage.

prompts.question: Some people think that children should not be allowed to use smartphones until they are 16. Do you agree or disagree?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words with a clear topic sentence and at least one example.

prompts.question: Artificial intelligence will replace many jobs in the next 20 years. Do the advantages of this trend outweigh the disadvantages?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words. Use topic sentence + explanation + example + result.

prompts.question: Some people believe that individuals can do little to reduce climate change and that only governments and big companies can make a difference. To what extent do you agree?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words. Stay focused on one side of the argument.

prompts.question: Plastic waste is becoming a serious problem in many countries. What are the causes, and what can be done to solve this problem?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words focusing on one cause or one solution.

prompts.question: Some people think that working from home is better than working in an office. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words with a clear topic sentence, explanation, and example.

prompts.question: Some companies have started a four-day working week. Do the advantages of this change outweigh the disadvantages?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words. Include a topic sentence, explanation, example, and result.

prompts.question: Some people believe that schools should give students more time for physical activities, even if this reduces academic study time. Do you agree or disagree?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words. Use a clear topic sentence and at least one example.

prompts.question: Fast food is now widely available in many countries. What are the effects of this on individuals and society, and what can be done about it?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words on either the effects or one possible solution.

prompts.question: Some people think that governments should spend more money on public transport rather than on building new roads. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words with topic sentence, explanation, example, and result.

prompts.question: Some people believe that governments should make voting compulsory in national elections. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words focusing on one of the two views.

prompts.question: In many countries, fewer young people are getting married. What are the causes of this trend, and what effects does it have on society?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words on one cause or one effect.

prompts.question: Some people believe that volunteer work should be a required part of high school education. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words with a clear topic sentence and example.

prompts.question: Some people think that long prison sentences are the most effective way to reduce crime. Others believe that education and rehabilitation work better. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words on one of the two views.

prompts.question: Online crime, such as identity theft and fraud, is increasing every year. What are the main causes of this problem, and what can governments and individuals do to reduce it?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words focusing on one cause or one solution.

prompts.question: Traffic congestion is becoming a serious problem in many large cities. What are the main causes, and how can it be reduced?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words on one cause or one solution.

prompts.question: Some people think that the best way to reduce pollution is to make private car use more expensive. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words with topic sentence, explanation, example, and result.

prompts.question: Some people think that the news media should focus more on positive stories instead of negative events. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words focusing on one side of the argument.

prompts.question: Social media has changed how people communicate and access information. Do the benefits of social media outweigh the drawbacks?
prompts.instruction: Write one body paragraph of 80–120 words. Use topic sentence + explanation + example + result.
